rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 19, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. INTERVENOR NATIONAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF HARTFORD, PACIFIC INDEMNITY COMPANY, FEDERAL INSURANCE COMPANY, HARTFORD ACCIDENT AND INDEMNITY COMPANY, AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY, CENTURY INDEMNITY COMPANY AS SUCCESSOR TO CCI's Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention. Hartford Accident Indemnity Company, American Guarantee and Liability Insurance Company, Century Indemnity Company as successor to CCI Insurance Company as successor to Insurance Company of North America, National Fire Insurance Company of Hartford successor by merger to Transcontinental Insurance Company, Pacific Indemnity Company, and Federal Insurance Company's motion for leave to file complaint in intervention is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
